The physical or process parameter being measured by the instrument (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow, voltage, torque, dimension, weight). Determines the applicable calibration standard and tolerance regime.

The expanded measurement uncertainty (k=2, 95% confidence) associated with the calibration result, expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 accredited calibration certificates.
The unit of measure applicable to as_found_reading, as_left_reading, reference_value, and tolerance fields (e.g., °C, bar, mA, mm, Nm, V, kg). Must conform to SI units per ISO 80000.
reference: ISO 80000 (SI Units); ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; NIST SP 811

description: Indicates whether the instrument has been placed out of service as a result of this calibration event (e.g., due to a fail or out-of-tolerance result). When True, the instrument must be quarantined and a CAPA initiated before return to service.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process; Maximo Asset Management

description: Assessment of whether measurements taken by this instrument since its last calibration may have been affected by the out-of-tolerance condition found. Required for product conformity risk assessment when a calibration failure is detected.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

description: The true or nominal value against which the instrument reading is compared during calibration. Established by the reference standard and used to compute as-found and as-left errors.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; NIST Handbook 44; ANSI Z540.3

description: Certificate number of the reference standard used during calibration, establishing the NIST/SI traceability chain. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 compliance documentation.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.7; NIST Traceability Policy; ANSI Z540.3

description: The qualification or certification number of the technician who performed the calibration, as issued by an accredited body (e.g., ASQ, NIST, internal competency register). Required for ISO/IEC 17025 personnel traceability.

Full name of the qualified technician who performed and certified the calibration. Captured as a denormalized field on the calibration certificate for regulatory traceability even if the technician record changes.

The minimum acceptable deviation from the reference value for the instrument to be considered in calibration. Expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit. Falling below this limit results in a fail or out-of-tolerance status.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI Z540.3; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6

description: The maximum acceptable deviation from the reference value for the instrument to be considered in calibration. Expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit. Exceeding this limit results in a fail or out-of-tolerance status.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ANSI Z540.3; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6

description: The timestamp when this calibration record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer ingestion pipeline.
Unique surrogate identifier for each condition monitoring or meter reading record. Primary key for the condition_reading data product in the asset domain.

description: Reference to a downtime event record if this reading was captured in the context of or immediately preceding an equipment downtime event. Enables correlation of condition readings with downtime causation for MTBF/MTTR analysis.

description: Reference to the physical asset or equipment from which this reading was captured. Links to the asset master record in Maximo Asset Management.

description: Reference to the physical plant location or functional location where the asset and sensor are installed at the time of reading. Supports geographic and facility-level aggregation of condition data.

description: Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule record that was triggered or updated by this reading. Populated when pm_trigger_flag is true.

description: Reference to the production line or manufacturing cell on which the asset is operating at the time of reading. Enables correlation of condition readings with production output, OEE, and downtime events.

description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Condition readings (vibration, temperature, pressure) captured during production are linked to the active work order for SPC, process quality control, and batch genealogy traceability — a standard req

Reference to the production shift during which this reading was captured. Enables shift-level analysis of equipment condition trends and correlation with operator-specific maintenance practices.

description: Severity classification assigned to this reading when a threshold breach is detected. description: The ambient environmental temperature in degrees Celsius at the measurement location at the time of reading. Used as a contextual covariate for temperature-sensitive condition measurements such as bearing temperature and oil viscosity.

description: The externally visible physical tag or barcode number affixed to the equipment from which this reading was taken. Provides a human-readable cross-reference to the physical asset in the field, independent of the system surrogate key.

description: Identifier of the data ingestion batch or pipeline run that loaded this reading into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reprocessing, and audit trail for the condition monitoring data pipeline.

description: The date and time when this condition reading record was first created or ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Distinct from reading_timestamp which captures the real-world event time.

Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this reading, computed by the IIoT gateway, SCADA historian, or data ingestion pipeline. description: The incremental change in the reading value since the previous reading for the same sensor or meter. description: For vibration and acoustic emission readings, the frequency band or spectral range (e.g., '10-1000 Hz', '1-10 kHz') within which the measurement was taken. Enables frequency-domain analysis for bearing fault detection and rotating machinery diagnostics.

Alphanumeric code identifying the functional location (plant area, production line, or equipment position) within the facility hierarchy where the reading was taken. Aligns with SAP PM functional location structure and Maximo location hierarchy.

description: The percentage of rated capacity or load at which the asset was operating when this reading was taken (0.00–100.00). Load normalization is essential for accurate condition trending — the same vibration amplitude at 50% load vs 100% load has different diagnostic significance.

The physical axis or direction in which the measurement was taken for directional sensors such as vibration accelerometers (axial, radial, tangential) or displacement probes. Null for non-directional measurements such as temperature or pressure.

Specific physical or operational quantity being measured. Examples include vibration, temperature, pressure, oil_viscosity, acoustic_emission, ultrasound, runtime_hours, cycle_count, energy_consumption_kwh, distance_km, production_units. description: Free-text or coded reason provided by the operator when manually overriding or correcting an automated reading. Required when is_manual_override is true. Supports audit trail and data quality governance.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this reading caused a meter-based or condition-based preventive maintenance (PM) schedule to be triggered in the CMMS. True when the cumulative meter value crossed a PM due-point or a condition indicator breached a CBM trigger threshold.
reference: Maximo Asset Management PM Scheduling; ISO 55000; TPM

description: The unprocessed, uncalibrated value as received directly from the sensor or IIoT gateway before any engineering unit conversion, calibration offset, or data quality correction is applied. Preserved for audit, recalibration, and data lineage purposes.

description: Origin system or method by which the reading was captured. Critical for data lineage and trust scoring.
reference: Aveva SCADA; Siemens Opcenter MES; Maximo Asset Management; IIoT

description: Quality or lifecycle status of the reading record. description: The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the condition measurement or meter reading was recorded. Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a condition indicator (instantaneous measurement such as vibration, temperature, pressure) or a cumulative meter/counter reading (runtime hours, cycle counts, energy consumption, distance traveled). Drives downstream PM trigger logic.

description: The principal observed numeric measurement or meter count captured at the reading timestamp. description: Reference to the specific sensor, transducer, or meter instrument that produced this reading. Identifies the IIoT device, SCADA tag, or manual meter point.

description: The date and time when this condition reading record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, for example following a manual override, data quality correction, or status update.

description: Current lifecycle status of the job plan indicating whether it is available for use on work orders and PM schedules. DRAFT = under development; ACTIVE = approved and in use; INACTIVE = temporarily suspended; PENDING_REVIEW = awaiting approval; OBSOLETE = superseded and retired.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Status; ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5

description: Classification of the maintenance activity type this job plan covers. Drives scheduling logic, resource allocation, and KPI reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PREVENTIVE|CORRECTIVE|PREDICTIVE|INSPECTION|OVERHAUL|LUBRICATION|CALIBRATION|EMERGENCY|SHUTDOWN — promote to reference product]
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Type; ISO 55001 Asset Management; ANSI/ISA-18.2

description: Maintenance planner group or planning team responsible for maintaining and executing this job plan (e.g., MECH-PLANNER-01, ELEC-PLANNER-NORTH). Aligns with SAP PM Planner Group and Maximo planning organization structure.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Planner Group; SAP PM Planner Group

description: Comma-separated list or narrative description of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for executing this job plan (e.g., safety glasses, hard hat, arc flash suit, chemical gloves, hearing protection). Mandatory for ISO 45001 and OSHA compliance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart I - Personal Protective Equipment; ISO 45001:2018; ANSI Z87.1

description: The primary labor craft or trade skill required to execute this job plan (e.g., Millwright, Electrician, Instrumentation Technician, Mechanic). Sourced from Maximo Craft master and used for work order labor planning.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Craft; SAP PM Work Center Capacity

description: Default priority level assigned to work orders generated from this job plan. Drives scheduling urgency and resource allocation in the CMMS. CRITICAL = safety/production-critical; HIGH = significant impact; MEDIUM = moderate impact; LOW = minor impact; ROUTINE = standard scheduled.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Priority; SAP PM Priority; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Reference to applicable regulatory standards, codes, or compliance requirements that this job plan satisfies (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 45001, OSHA 1910.147, IEC 62443, UL certification requirement). Supports compliance audit traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015; ISO 45001:2018; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 62443; UL Standards; CE Marking Directive

description: Minimum number of technicians required to safely and effectively execute this job plan. Used for crew scheduling and compliance with safety regulations (e.g., confined space entry requiring a standby person per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146).
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 - General Industry Safety; Maximo Job Plan Crew Size

description: List of specialized tools, equipment, and instruments required to execute this job plan (e.g., torque wrench, vibration analyzer, multimeter, alignment laser, hydraulic jack). Used for tool reservation and kitting in the CMMS.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Tools; SAP PM Task List Tools

description: Version or revision identifier of the job plan, incremented each time the procedure is formally updated (e.g., REV-01, REV-02). Supports change control and audit traceability per ISO 9001 document control requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5 - Documented Information; Maximo Job Plan Revision

description: Narrative description of mandatory safety precautions, lockout/tagout (LOTO) requirements, personal protective equipment (PPE), and hazard controls that must be observed before and during execution of this job plan. Compliance with OSHA and ISO 45001 requirements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 - Lockout/Tagout; ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; ANSI Z244.1

description: Indicates whether execution of this job plan requires a planned production shutdown or equipment isolation. Used for maintenance window scheduling and production planning coordination to minimize OEE impact.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Shutdown Required; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness); ISO 55001

description: Plant or facility site code where this job plan is applicable. Enables site-specific job plan management in multi-site manufacturing operations. Aligns with SAP Plant code and Maximo Site.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Site; SAP S/4HANA Plant Code; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Operational system of record from which this job plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer. MAXIMO = IBM Maximo Asset Management; SAP_PM = SAP Plant Maintenance; MANUAL = manually entered; TEAMCENTER = Siemens Teamcenter PLM; OPCENTER = Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: Informatica MDM - Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Governance

description: Natural key or identifier of this job plan record in the originating source system (e.g., Maximo Job Plan Number, SAP PM Task List Number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.
reference: Informatica MDM - Source System Key; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage

description: Narrative or structured list of spare parts and materials required for this job plan, including part numbers and quantities (e.g., 'Bearing SKF 6205-2RS x2, O-Ring Kit x1, Filter Element x1'). Used for pre-kitting and MRP-driven spare parts reservation.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Materials; SAP MM Material Requirements Planning (MRP); SAP PM Planned Materials

description: Total number of discrete task steps defined within this job plan. Provides a quick indicator of procedure complexity and is used for work order progress tracking in Maximo and Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Tasks; SAP PM Task List Operations

description: Human-readable descriptive title of the job plan summarizing the maintenance activity (e.g., 'Annual Preventive Maintenance - CNC Spindle Assembly', 'Hydraulic System Inspection'). Used in work order templates and PM schedules.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Description; SAP PM Task List Short Text

description: Trade or discipline category of the maintenance work defined in this job plan. Used to route work orders to the correct craft group and for workforce planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MECHANICAL|ELECTRICAL|INSTRUMENTATION|CIVIL|HVAC|HYDRAULIC|PNEUMATIC|LUBRICATION|AUTOMATION|WELDING — promote to reference product]
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Work Category; SAP PM Work Center

description: Maintenance work center or department responsible for executing this job plan (e.g., MAINT-MECH-01, MAINT-ELEC-02). Maps to SAP PM Work Center and Maximo Labor Group for capacity planning and cost center assignment.
reference: SAP PM Work Center; Maximo Asset Management - Labor Group; SAP CO Cost Center

description: Default work order type that should be generated when this job plan is applied. PM = Preventive Maintenance; CM = Corrective Maintenance; EM = Emergency Maintenance; INSPECTION = Inspection only; OVERHAUL = Major overhaul; PROJECT = Capital project work.
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Type; SAP PM Order Type

business_glossary_term: Job Plan Material Requirement - Job Plan Material Requirement Id
description: Primary key for the job_plan_material_requirement association

business_glossary_term: Job Plan Material Requirement - Job Plan Id
description: Foreign key linking to the parent job plan (task list) that defines this material requirement.

business_glossary_term: Job Plan Material Requirement - Spare Part Id
description: Foreign key linking to the spare part (material) required by this job plan.

Estimated cost of this specific spare part line within the job plan BOM, calculated as quantity_required multiplied by the standard unit cost at planning time. Contributes to the job plan header estimated_material_cost rollup.

Estimated total cost of spare parts and materials required to execute this job plan. Used for maintenance budget planning and procurement forecasting. Expressed in the operational currency. [Moved from job_plan: This is a derived rollup of all job_plan_material_requirement.estimated_cost lines. While a header-level summary may be retained on job_plan for convenience, the source of truth for material cost is the individual requirement lines in this association. The denormalized spare_parts_list STRING (referenced in detection reasoning) must be removed entirely and replaced by this association table.]
reference: Maximo Asset Management - Estimated Material Cost; SAP PM Planned Materials; IFRS/GAAP

description: Indicates whether this spare part is mandatory for job plan execution (true) or optional/conditional based on findings during the task (false). Belongs to the requirement relationship.
Sequence number defining the order in which this material is staged or consumed within the job plan task steps. Belongs to the requirement relationship, not to either entity alone.
Quantity of this spare part required per single execution of the job plan. Belongs to the requirement relationship, not to the part master or the job plan header.
Unique system-generated identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule record. Primary key for the pm_schedule data product.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule Object

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Preventive Maintenance Contracts: links maintenance schedules to the customer contract holder for compliance reporting and contract billing.

Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: PM schedules for equipment under OEM or third-party maintenance contracts must reference the governing service agreement to ensure maintenance intervals and scope comply with contractual obligations —
Reference to the most recently generated work order from this PM schedule. Enables direct navigation from the schedule to the latest execution record for status tracking.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Last Work Order; SAP S/4HANA PM Order

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Preventive‑maintenance schedules are created per component type to align with design‑life expectations.

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules in industrial manufacturing are frequently defined and billed under service contracts. The sales_contract governs PM scope, frequency, SLA uptime targets, and billing
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SCHEDULING: PM schedules generate labor/material spend that must be posted to the responsible cost center.
Reference to the physical equipment or asset for which this PM schedule is defined. Links to the asset master record (e.g., CNC machine, PLC, conveyor, robotic arm).
reference: Maximo Asset Management — Asset Object; ISO 55001:2014 Asset Management

description: Reference to the job plan that defines the detailed task steps, materials, tools, and labor requirements for this PM schedule. Job plans are maintained in Maximo and reused across multiple PM schedules.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — Job Plan Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.1 Operational Planning

description: Reference to the operational location (plant, building, production line, or functional location) where the asset subject to this PM schedule resides.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — Location Object; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location

description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: PM schedules for customer-installed equipment are governed by SLA agreements (preventive_maintenance_included flag). Direct link enables SLA-driven PM schedule generation and compliance verification a

business_glossary_term: Pm Planned Part - Spare Part Id
description: Foreign key linking to the spare part master record required by this PM schedule
Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Preventive‑maintenance contracts often involve third‑party service providers; linking enables contract compliance reporting.

Name or employee identifier of the maintenance manager or engineering authority who approved this PM schedule for execution. Required for regulatory compliance and quality management system documentation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2 Creating and Updating; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 5.3 Roles and Responsibilities

description: Date on which this PM schedule was formally approved by the authorized maintenance manager or engineering authority. Marks the schedule as valid for work order generation.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; Maximo Asset Management — PM Approval Workflow

description: Detailed narrative description of the preventive maintenance schedule, including the scope of work, maintenance objectives, and any special instructions or safety precautions relevant to the task.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Description; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Unit of measure for the condition monitoring threshold value (e.g., mm/s for vibration velocity, °C for temperature, cSt for oil viscosity). Provides context for interpreting condition_threshold_value.
reference: ISO 13373 Condition Monitoring; IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standards

description: Numeric threshold value for condition-based PM triggering. When the monitored parameter (e.g., vibration amplitude, temperature, oil viscosity) exceeds this value, a PM work order is generated. Applicable when trigger_type is CONDITION.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — Condition Monitoring; ISO 13373 Condition Monitoring of Machines; IIoT Sensor Integration

description: Timestamp when this PM schedule record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Informatica MDM Data Governance

description: Number of maintenance technicians or craft workers required to execute this PM task simultaneously. Used for labor resource planning and shift scheduling.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Labor Requirements; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 7.1

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated_material_cost field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Date after which this PM schedule is no longer active and will not generate new work orders. Null for open-ended schedules. Used when an asset is decommissioned or a maintenance program is superseded.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Effective End Date; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 8.1

description: Date from which this PM schedule becomes active and eligible to generate work orders. Supports phased rollout of new maintenance programs and asset commissioning workflows.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Effective Date; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 8.1

description: Estimated number of hours the asset will be unavailable for production during execution of this PM task. Used in OEE calculations, production scheduling, and downtime planning.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Downtime; OEE Framework; ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing

description: Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the preventive maintenance task. Used for maintenance scheduling, craft resource planning, and downtime window estimation.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Estimated Duration; SAP S/4HANA PM Order Duration

description: Estimated cost of materials and spare parts required to execute this PM task, in the local operating currency. Used for maintenance budget planning and CapEx/OpEx cost allocation.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Cost Estimate; SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Controlling; IFRS IAS 16

description: Unit of measure for the PM recurrence frequency. Combined with frequency_value to define the full interval (e.g., 30 DAYS, 500 HOURS, 10000 CYCLES). Determines whether the schedule is calendar-based or meter-based. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAYS|WEEKS|MONTHS|YEARS|HOURS|CYCLES|KILOMETERS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency Unit; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

description: Numeric value representing how often the PM task should recur. Interpreted in conjunction with frequency_unit (e.g., a value of 30 with unit DAYS means every 30 days; a value of 500 with unit HOURS means every 500 operating hours).
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

description: Indicates whether this spare part is critical for PM execution — i.e., the PM task cannot be safely or effectively completed without this part being available. Drives pre-staging and kitting priority decisions.

Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by a regulatory body (e.g., OSHA, EPA, UL, CE Marking authority) and must be completed and documented for compliance purposes. Drives audit trail requirements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; EPA Regulations; UL Certification Requirements; CE Marking Directive

description: Indicates whether this PM schedule is classified as safety-critical, meaning non-execution could result in personnel injury, environmental incident, or regulatory violation. Safety-critical PMs require mandatory completion tracking.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 General Industry Standards

description: Date on which the most recent execution of this PM schedule was completed and the corresponding work order was closed. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due date.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Last Completion Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1 Monitoring

description: Date on which this PM schedule definition was last revised or updated. Supports document control, audit trails, and change management processes.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Maximo Asset Management — PM Revision History

description: Number of additional buffer days beyond the spare part's standard lead time that the planner has specified for this PM-part combination. Accounts for PM-specific procurement complexity or criticality. Belongs to the relationship, not to the part master or PM schedule alone.
reference: Maximo PM planned parts lead time override

description: Number of days before the next_due_date that the PM work order should be generated to allow sufficient time for parts procurement, scheduling, and preparation. Supports JIT maintenance planning.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Lead Time; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 8.1

description: Classification of the maintenance activity type. PREVENTIVE: time/usage-based task to prevent failure. PREDICTIVE: condition-monitoring-driven task. INSPECTION: regulatory or quality inspection. LUBRICATION: lubrication task. CALIBRATION: instrument calibration. OVERHAUL: major scheduled overhaul.
reference: ISO 13306:2017 Maintenance Terminology; Maximo Asset Management — PM Type

description: Unit of measure for the meter reading used in meter-based PM scheduling (e.g., HOURS for operating hours, CYCLES for press strokes, KILOMETERS for vehicle mileage). Null when trigger_type is CALENDAR only.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — Meter Object; ISO 55001:2014

description: Calculated date on which the next preventive maintenance work order is due to be generated or executed. Derived from last_completion_date plus the frequency interval. Drives maintenance scheduling and resource planning.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Next Due Date; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

description: Meter reading value (e.g., operating hours, cycle count, kilometers) at which the next PM work order is due. Applicable when trigger_type is METER or when a meter-based override supplements calendar scheduling.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Meter Due; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

description: Business priority assigned to this PM schedule, used to rank and sequence work order generation when competing maintenance demands exist. CRITICAL schedules are associated with safety-critical or regulatory-mandated equipment.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Priority; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 6.1 Risk Assessment

description: The quantity of this spare part required to complete one full execution of the associated PM schedule. Belongs to the relationship — the same part may be required in different quantities on different PM schedules.

Specific regulatory standard, clause, or requirement that mandates this PM schedule (e.g., 'OSHA 29 CFR 1910.217 Mechanical Power Presses', 'ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 Monitoring Resources'). Populated when is_regulatory_required is True.
reference: OSHA; EPA; ISO 9001:2015; IEC 62443; ANSI Standards

description: Document revision number for this PM schedule, incremented when the schedule definition is updated (e.g., frequency change, task scope change, job plan update). Supports document control and change management per ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 Control of Documented Information; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

description: Human-readable name or title describing the preventive maintenance schedule (e.g., 'Monthly Lubrication — CNC Spindle', 'Annual Safety Inspection — Conveyor Line 3'). Used for display and search.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the PM schedule, as assigned in Maximo Asset Management. Used for cross-system reference and field communication (e.g., PM-2024-00123).
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Current lifecycle status of the preventive maintenance schedule. ACTIVE schedules generate work orders automatically. SUSPENDED schedules are temporarily paused (e.g., during planned shutdown). CLOSED schedules are retired.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: The sequential position in the PM job plan task list at which this spare part is consumed or installed. Enables technicians to stage parts in the correct order for efficient PM execution. Belongs to the PM-part relationship, not to either entity independently.

Ordering sequence number for this PM schedule within a TPM task sequence or maintenance route. Enables ordered execution of multiple PM tasks on the same asset or production line during a maintenance window.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Sequence; TPM Pillar Framework — Planned Maintenance

description: Indicates whether the asset must be taken offline (production shutdown) to perform this PM task. Critical for production planning and scheduling maintenance windows to minimize OEE impact.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Shutdown Required; Siemens Opcenter MES Scheduling; ISO 55001:2014

description: Indicates whether this PM task requires spare parts or consumable materials to be reserved or procured prior to execution. Triggers inventory reservation in WMS or SAP MM when True.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Materials; SAP S/4HANA MM Materials Management; ISO 55001:2014

description: Allowable number of days before or after the next_due_date within which the PM task can be performed without being considered overdue. Provides scheduling flexibility while maintaining compliance.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Tolerance; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: TPM pillar classification to which this PM schedule belongs. Aligns maintenance activities with the eight TPM pillars: Autonomous Maintenance, Planned Maintenance, Quality Maintenance, Focused Improvement, Early Equipment Management, Education and Training, Safety Health and Environment, Office TPM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUTONOMOUS|PLANNED|QUALITY|FOCUSED|EARLY_EQUIPMENT|EDUCATION|SAFETY|OFFICE — promote to reference product]
reference: TPM Institute — Total Productive Maintenance Pillar Framework; JIPM TPM Award Criteria

description: Mechanism that triggers the generation of a work order for this PM schedule. CALENDAR: time-based (e.g., every 30 days). METER: usage-based (e.g., every 500 operating hours). CONDITION: condition-based monitoring threshold breach. SEASONAL: tied to seasonal calendar. EVENT: triggered by a specific operational event.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Trigger Type; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2; TPM Pillar Framework

description: Timestamp when this PM schedule record was last modified in the system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Informatica MDM Data Governance

description: Code identifying the maintenance work center or crew responsible for executing this PM schedule (e.g., MAINT-ELEC-01, MAINT-MECH-02). Used for capacity planning and work order routing in SAP PM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Work Center; Maximo Asset Management — Crew/Work Group

description: Cumulative count of work orders that have been generated from this PM schedule since its creation. Provides a historical activity indicator and supports MTBF/MTTR analysis.
reference: Maximo Asset Management — PM Work Order History; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 9.1

description: Primary key for the affected_item association
Foreign key linking to the Component master record that is affected by the engineering change.
Foreign key linking to the Engineering Change Order that governs this affected item record.

The required action for existing inventory and work-in-progress of this specific component under this ECO. Belongs to the affected item relationship, not to the ECO globally or the component master, as disposition varies per component per change.

The date on which this specific component change becomes effective for use in production. May differ from the ECO-level effectivity date when components within the same ECO have staggered implementation schedules.

The current revision level of this component before the engineering change is applied. Captured per affected item because different components on the same ECO may be transitioning from different revision levels.
reference: ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: Actual date when the engineering change was implemented for this specific component in production systems. Captured per affected item for traceability and PPAP compliance.
Current implementation status of the change for this specific component. Tracked per affected item to allow partial ECO closure when some components are implemented ahead of others.

The quantity of this component in existing inventory or WIP that is subject to the disposition action. Belongs to the affected item relationship as it is specific to the ECO-component pairing at the time of change.
The target revision level of this component after the engineering change is applied. Defines the post-change revision state for this specific component-ECO pairing.
reference: ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

description: Unique identifier for the engineering change notice record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: ECNs in industrial manufacturing require formal customer notification and approval workflows (ecn.customer_notification_required flag exists but has no FK target). Linking ECN to customer_account iden

Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: ECN-driven AVL governance: engineering changes that affect part form/fit/function require AVL re-evaluation. Linking ECN to the impacted AVL entry enables sourcing teams to trigger re-qualification an

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ECNs have cost_impact_estimate and cost_impact_currency. In industrial manufacturing, ECN cost impacts are charged to cost centers for financial accountability. ECN is distinct from ECO — it is the fo
Reference to the parent engineering change order that was approved and is now being communicated via this ECN.

Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: ECNs directly affect material masters in ERP — when an ECN is released, the material master revision level and effectivity are updated. Change management processes require knowing the primary material

Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: ECNs with bom_impact_flag or routing_impact_flag affect specific production lines. Change impact assessment requires knowing which production lines must implement the ECN. Enables production line chan

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: ECN Supplier Notification process: when `supplier_notification_required=true`, procurement must track which supplier receives the ECN. A manufacturing domain expert expects ECNs to be directed at spec
Number of stakeholders who have formally acknowledged receipt and understanding of this ECN. Used to track distribution completion.
Indicates whether recipients must formally acknowledge receipt and understanding of this ECN. True = acknowledgement tracking enabled; False = informational only.
Total number of stakeholders required to acknowledge this ECN before it can be marked as fully distributed.
Total number of engineering drawings, Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models, or technical documents affected by this ECN.
Total number of distinct part numbers or Bill of Materials (BOM) items affected by this ECN.
Comma-separated list or narrative of product lines, families, or platforms impacted by this ECN. Used for scoping and impact analysis.
Date when the ECN was formally approved for release by the designated approver or change control board.
Indicates whether this ECN requires changes to one or more Bill of Materials (BOM) structures. True = BOM changes required; False = no BOM impact.

Primary category of the engineering change. Design = product design modification; Material = material substitution; Process = manufacturing process change; Documentation = drawing/spec update; Specification = requirement change; Supplier = vendor change.
Detailed narrative description of the engineering change being communicated, including technical modifications, affected components, and implementation instructions.
Business justification and rationale for the engineering change, including root cause, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, or quality improvement drivers.
Date when the ECN was closed after successful implementation and acknowledgement by all required stakeholders.
Additional notes, instructions, or clarifications related to the ECN implementation, distribution, or acknowledgement process.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost impact estimate (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

Estimated total cost impact of implementing this ECN, including material, labor, tooling, and inventory disposition costs. Positive values indicate cost increase; negative values indicate cost savings.
Timestamp when this ECN record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether customers must be notified of this ECN due to form, fit, function, or regulatory changes. True = customer notification required; False = internal only.
Comma-separated list or structured text of roles, departments, or individuals who must receive and acknowledge this ECN (e.g., Production, Quality, Supply Chain, Engineering).
Business identifier for the engineering change notice, typically formatted as ECN-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and tracking across systems.

Current lifecycle status of the ECN. Draft = being prepared; Released = approved for distribution; Distributed = sent to stakeholders; Acknowledged = recipients confirmed receipt; Closed = implementation complete; Cancelled = voided.

Classification of the ECN based on urgency and scope. Standard = normal process; Urgent = expedited review; Emergency = immediate action required; Field Change = affects deployed products; Design Improvement = enhancement.
Date when the engineering change becomes effective and must be implemented in production, procurement, and quality systems.

Status of synchronization of this ECN to downstream Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (SAP S/4HANA). Pending = awaiting sync; Synced = successfully transmitted; Failed = sync error; Not Required = no ERP impact.
Actual date when the engineering change was fully implemented across all affected systems and processes.
Indicates whether this ECN affects existing inventory, requiring disposition decisions (scrap, rework, use-as-is). True = inventory action required; False = no inventory impact.

Status of synchronization of this ECN to Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for shop floor implementation. Pending = awaiting sync; Synced = successfully transmitted; Failed = sync error; Not Required = no MES impact.
Timestamp when this ECN record was last modified or updated.
Unique identifier for this ECN in the source Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for system integration and traceability.
Priority level for implementation of the ECN. Critical = safety/regulatory; High = customer impact; Medium = quality improvement; Low = documentation only.
Indicates whether this ECN affects regulatory compliance, certifications, or safety approvals (UL, CE, ISO). True = regulatory review required; False = no regulatory impact.
Date when the ECN was officially released and approved for distribution to affected stakeholders.
The previous revision level of the affected item(s) before this ECN takes effect. Used to track revision transitions.
The new revision level of the affected item(s) after this ECN is implemented. Represents the target state.
Indicates whether this ECN requires changes to manufacturing routings or process plans. True = routing changes required; False = no routing impact.
Indicates whether external suppliers must be notified of this ECN due to purchased part changes or specification updates. True = supplier notification required; False = internal only.
Unique identifier for the engineering change order record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑driven engineering change orders are tracked via ECO linked to the requesting customer_account for audit trails.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: ECOs consume engineering change budgets independently of projects. ECOs can exist without an engineering_project (standalone change orders). Direct ECO→finance_budget link enables budget vs. actual tr

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: ECOs affecting contracted products require customer approval per the sales contract terms. The eco table has customer_approval_required and customer_approval_date fields — linking to the specific sale

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ECOs carry actual_cost_impact and estimated_cost_impact fields. Engineering change management requires tracking ECO costs against cost centers for financial accountability and change program reporting

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Engineering Change Orders are initiated within the context of engineering projects. The eco table has `initiator_department` and project-related metadata but no FK to the governing project. Linking ec
Number of stakeholders who have acknowledged receipt of the ECN.
Indicates whether recipients of the ECN are required to formally acknowledge receipt and understanding.
Actual financial impact realized after implementation, including all direct and indirect costs.
Actual impact on production schedule in days realized after implementation.
Total number of product items (parts, assemblies, drawings, documents) affected by this engineering change.
Date when the ECO received final approval authorization to proceed with implementation.
Full name of the final approver who authorized the engineering change.
Job title or role of the final approver (e.g., Chief Engineer, VP Engineering, Quality Manager).
Business priority level for implementing the change. Critical = safety/regulatory; High = customer impact; Medium = quality improvement; Low = cost reduction.

Classification of the engineering change by category: design (product geometry/function), material (BOM substitution), process (manufacturing method), documentation (drawing/spec update), specification (performance criteria), or tooling (fixture/jig modification).
Date when the ECO was formally closed after successful implementation and verification.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost impact amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
Timestamp when this ECO record was first created in the system.
Date when customer approval was received for the engineering change.

Primary street address of the warehouse facility. First line of the physical location address.
Secondary address information such as building number, suite, or floor. Optional additional address detail.
Indicates whether the warehouse uses automated storage and retrieval systems (AS/RS). Impacts throughput rates, labor requirements, and integration with WMS and MES.
City or municipality where the warehouse facility is located.
Indicates whether the warehouse has climate control capabilities for temperature and humidity-sensitive materials. Critical for pharmaceutical, electronic component, and chemical storage compliance.
Primary email address for warehouse operational communication. Used for shipment notifications, inventory alerts, and administrative correspondence.
Primary contact phone number for the warehouse facility. Used for carrier coordination, emergency contact, and operational communication.
Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the warehouse is located. Used for customs, tax, and regulatory compliance.
Timestamp when the warehouse master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Indicates whether the warehouse is a customs-bonded facility authorized to store imported goods before duty payment. Critical for international supply chain and duty deferral strategies.
ERP system plant code associated with this warehouse. Links warehouse to manufacturing plant in SAP S/4HANA for MRP and production planning integration.
Classification of the warehouse based on the primary type of inventory stored. Determines storage requirements, handling procedures, and integration with MRP and MES systems.
Type of fire suppression system installed in the warehouse. Critical for insurance, safety compliance, and determining storage eligibility for flammable materials.
Indicates whether the warehouse is certified to store hazardous materials. Determines eligibility for storing chemicals, flammables, and regulated substances per OSHA and EPA requirements.
Comma-separated list of hazardous material storage classes the warehouse is certified to handle (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Based on UN hazard classification system.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: Indicates whether the warehouse facility holds ISO 14001 environmental management system certification. Demonstrates commitment to environmental sustainability and regulatory compliance.
Indicates whether the warehouse facility holds ISO 9001 quality management system certification. Impacts supplier qualification and customer audit requirements.
Timestamp when the warehouse master record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse facility. Used for logistics optimization, route planning, and TMS integration.
Date when the warehouse lease agreement expires. Null for owned facilities. Used for facility planning and contract renewal management.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse facility. Used for logistics optimization, route planning, and TMS integration.
Total count of loading and unloading dock positions. Determines receiving and shipping throughput capacity.
Total count of discrete storage bin locations within the warehouse. Used for bin location management and inventory tracking granularity in SAP WM.
Textual description of standard operating hours and shift patterns (e.g., 24/7, Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, Three-shift operation). Used for carrier scheduling and labor planning.
Current operational state of the warehouse facility. Determines availability for inventory allocation and order fulfillment operations.
Classification of warehouse ownership model. Determines financial accounting treatment, CapEx vs OpEx allocation, and operational control level.
Postal or ZIP code for the warehouse facility address. Used for logistics routing and carrier integration.
Identifier of the source system that created or owns this warehouse master record (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, DYNAMICS_365_SCM, MDM). Used for data lineage and system-of-record identification.
Classification of physical security measures and access controls at the warehouse. Determines eligibility for storing high-value goods, controlled substances, and export-controlled items.
State, province, or regional administrative division where the warehouse is located.
Floor area dedicated to inventory storage in square meters. Excludes receiving docks, shipping areas, and administrative space.
Maximum temperature that can be maintained in climate-controlled zones, measured in Celsius. Used for material storage requirement validation.
Minimum temperature that can be maintained in climate-controlled zones, measured in Celsius. Used for material storage requirement validation.
IANA time zone identifier for the warehouse location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin). Used for scheduling, shift planning, and cross-facility coordination.
Total storage capacity of the warehouse measured in cubic meters. Represents maximum volumetric storage capability for inventory planning and capacity utilization analysis.
Total floor area of the warehouse facility in square meters. Includes storage, receiving, shipping, and administrative areas.
Actual usable storage capacity after accounting for aisles, equipment, and operational space. Used for realistic inventory allocation and safety stock calculations.
Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the warehouse facility. Used in SAP WM and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM for warehouse identification and cross-system integration.
Full business name of the warehouse facility. Human-readable identifier used in operational documentation and reporting.
Code identifying the WMS system deployed at this warehouse (e.g., SAP_WM, DYNAMICS_365_SCM, MANHATTAN). Used for system integration routing and data synchronization.

Secondary address information (suite, building).
Maximum electrical output capacity of the plant in megawatts.

Date the plant was permanently shut down, if applicable.

Total electricity consumption for the reporting period in megawatt‑hours.

Higher‑level region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for reporting.
Number of recorded safety incidents in the reporting period.

Quantity of waste produced by the plant in metric tons.

Unique identifier for the production line. Primary key for the production line master entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production line overhead is budgeted and reported per cost center; the link supports line‑level cost reporting.

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Production lines in industrial manufacturing are typically dedicated to specific product families (e.g., switchgear assembly line, motor winding line). This link drives capacity planning by product fa
Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Needed to associate each production line with its plant location for capacity planning and regulatory compliance.

Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: A production line is physically located within a plant. This is a critical missing master data relationship — production lines are plant-specific assets and all capacity, OEE, and throughput reporting

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Production lines are assigned to profit centers for line-level profitability reporting in industrial manufacturing. Controllers use production line-to-profit-center mapping for contribution margin ana

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Production lines are commissioned, upgraded, or decommissioned as part of engineering capital projects. Linking production lines to their governing engineering project enables project cost tracking, c

Most recent calculated Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production line based on actual performance data.
Degree of automation implemented on the production line, ranging from manual operations to fully automated lights-out manufacturing.
reference: IEC 62443 Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security

description: Indicates whether this production line is identified as a bottleneck or capacity constraint in the manufacturing process requiring special attention in scheduling.
reference: Theory of Constraints, Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)

description: Total time from last good piece of previous run to first good piece of new run, including setup and stabilization time.
Date when the production line was first commissioned and put into operational service.
reference: Asset Lifecycle Management, ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Timestamp when this production line master record was first created in the system.
Actual average time required to complete one production cycle on this line. Used to compare against takt time for performance analysis.
Identifier of the source system from which this production line master data originated or is synchronized.
reference: Informatica MDM Master Data Management, Data Lineage

description: Theoretical maximum production rate of the line measured in units per hour under ideal conditions. Used for capacity planning and OEE calculations.
Average energy consumption in kilowatt-hours required to produce one unit of output on this production line. Used for energy management and cost analysis.
Indicates whether this production line is subject to environmental compliance monitoring and reporting requirements.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, EPA Environmental Protection Agency

business_glossary_term: ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) Work Center Code
description: Corresponding work center code in the ERP system for integration with production planning and costing modules.
reference: SAP PP Production Planning Work Center

description: Date of the most recent major upgrade or modernization performed on the production line equipment or control systems.
reference: Asset Lifecycle Management, CapEx Capital Expenditure Tracking

description: Timestamp when this production line master record was most recently updated or modified.
Reference URL or document path to the physical layout diagram or CAD drawing of the production line configuration.
reference: CAD Computer-Aided Design, PLM Product Lifecycle Management

description: Unique business identifier code for the production line used across MES and ERP systems. Externally-known identifier for scheduling, capacity planning, and shop floor control.
reference: SAP PP Production Planning, Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Human-readable name of the production line for identification and reporting purposes.
Classification of the production line based on its primary manufacturing function. Determines the type of operations performed on this line.
Unique identifier for this production line in the Manufacturing Execution System used for shop floor control and production tracking.
Average time between equipment failures on this production line, used for reliability analysis and preventive maintenance planning.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data, TPM Total Productive Maintenance

description: Average time required to repair and restore this production line to operational status after a failure event.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data, TPM Total Productive Maintenance

description: Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational considerations specific to this production line.
Total count of work stations or process steps configured on this production line. Defines the line configuration and complexity.
Current operational state of the production line indicating availability for production scheduling and capacity planning.
reference: ISO 22400 Key Performance Indicators for Manufacturing Operations Management

description: Total hours per day that the production line is scheduled to be available for production, excluding planned maintenance windows.
Planned date for decommissioning or retirement of this production line from active service.
reference: Asset Lifecycle Management, ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Indicates whether output from this production line requires mandatory quality inspection before release to inventory.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management, SAP QM Quality Management

description: List of safety certifications or compliance requirements applicable to this production line based on the products manufactured and jurisdictional regulations.
reference: OSHA Occupational Safety and Health, ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety, CE Marking

business_glossary_term: SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) System Tag
description: Unique tag identifier in the SCADA system used to monitor and control this production line in real-time.
reference: Aveva SCADA Process Control, IEC 61131 PLC Programming

description: Average time required to change over the production line from one product or batch to another, including tooling changes and adjustments.
reference: SMED Single-Minute Exchange of Die, ISO 22400

description: Operating shift pattern configured for this production line defining daily operational hours and crew rotation.
Reference URL or document path to the standard operating procedures governing operation of this production line.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, Document Management

description: Target time interval between completion of successive units, calculated as available production time divided by customer demand. Critical metric for lean manufacturing and production scheduling.
Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production line, representing the goal for availability, performance, and quality combined.
Unit of measure for the throughput rate indicating how production output is quantified for this line.
Unique identifier for the manufacturing routing. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Subcontract routing costing: external processing routing operations are priced and governed by supplier agreements. Linking routing to agreement enables standard cost calculation for subcontracted ste
Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the material components consumed by this routing. Links routing to BOM for integrated production planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BOM (Bill of Materials) Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Routings define standard costs per operation assigned to cost centers for activity-based costing and standard cost calculation. In industrial manufacturing product costing, routing-to-cost-center assi

Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECNs with routing_impact_flag=true directly trigger routing updates. Traceability from the updated routing back to the ECN that caused the change is a standard engineering change management requiremen

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Routings are revision-controlled and must align with the engineering revision of the product. When an ECN changes a component, the routing is updated and must reference the new revision. Standard PLM/

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Routing operations reference engineering/process specifications for critical parameters (weld specs, torque values, surface finish). Process engineers link routings to governing specifications for ope
Reference to the material or finished good that this routing produces. Links to the material master in inventory domain.
Manufacturing plant or facility where this routing is executed. Links to plant master data.
FK to production.production_line

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Subcontracted operations: routing operations in industrial manufacturing can be externally processed (e.g., heat treatment, plating, machining). The routing must reference the subcontract supplier to

Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Subcontract operation site: external processing routing steps are performed at a specific supplier site, which determines lead time, capacity, and quality certification requirements. Site-level routin
Date on which this routing was approved for production use.

Current approval status of the routing. Pending routings await review; approved routings are authorized for production; rejected routings require rework; under_review routings are in the approval workflow.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this routing for production use.
Standard lot size or batch quantity for which the routing times and resource requirements are defined. All operation times are calculated relative to this base quantity.
Unit of measure for the base quantity. Standard ISO unit codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter).
Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Supports traceability of process changes.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost amount.
Sequential counter within the routing group. Together with routing_group, forms an alternative business key.
Timestamp when this routing record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this routing is the default routing for the material. True if this is the primary routing; false if it is an alternative.

Indicates whether this is a phantom routing that is automatically consumed without generating a separate production order. Used for subassemblies that are immediately incorporated into parent assemblies.
Timestamp when this routing record was last modified.
Most recent date on which this routing was used in a production order or work order. Used to identify obsolete or inactive routings.
Minimum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.
Maximum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Null indicates no upper limit.
Production planning team or planner responsible for maintaining and scheduling this routing.
Detailed textual description of the routing purpose, scope, and special instructions. Provides context for production planners and shop floor operators.
Grouping code for related routings. Used to organize routings by product family, process type, or manufacturing cell.
Business identifier for the routing. Externally visible routing code used in production planning and shop floor documentation.

Current lifecycle status of the routing. Draft routings are under development; released routings are approved for use; active routings are currently in production; inactive routings are temporarily suspended; obsolete routings are retired; blocked routings are prohibited from use.
Extended free-form text field for detailed routing notes, special handling instructions, safety warnings, and quality requirements.

Classification of the routing purpose. Production routings define standard manufacturing sequences; inspection routings define quality check sequences; rework routings define repair processes; universal routings apply across multiple materials; rate routings define continuous process flows; reference routings serve as templates.

Scheduling strategy for this routing. Forward scheduling starts from the earliest start date; backward scheduling works back from the required finish date; midpoint scheduling balances around a target date.

System of record from which this routing was sourced. SAP_PP indicates SAP S/4HANA Production Planning; TEAMCENTER indicates Siemens Teamcenter PLM; OPCENTER indicates Siemens Opcenter MES; MANUAL indicates manually entered routing.
Unique identifier of this routing in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Standard cost to execute this routing for the base quantity. Includes labor, machine, overhead, and tooling costs. Used for cost accounting and variance analysis.
Total cumulative labor time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Labor time may scale differently than machine time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Work Center Capacity Planning

description: Total cumulative lead time in hours required to complete all operations in this routing for the base quantity. Includes setup, machine, labor, and queue times.
reference: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) Lead Time Calculation

description: Total cumulative machine processing time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Machine time scales with lot size.
Total number of operations (steps) defined in this routing. Derived from routing operation line items.
Total cumulative setup time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Setup time is independent of lot size.

Intended application context for the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide backup processes; trial routings support pilot runs; prototype routings support R&D; emergency routings address contingency scenarios.
Total number of times this routing has been used in production orders or work orders. Indicates routing popularity and stability.
Effective start date from which this routing is valid and can be used in production planning and execution.
Effective end date after which this routing is no longer valid. Null indicates indefinite validity.
Version identifier for the routing. Supports versioning of routing definitions to track engineering changes and process improvements.
Primary key for the routing_operation association
Foreign key linking this operation step to its parent manufacturing routing header.
Foreign key linking this operation step to the work center where the operation is performed.

Control key defining how this specific operation is processed — whether it triggers confirmations, quality inspections, costing, or external processing. Operation-level override of the work center default control key.
Standard labor time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. May differ from machine time when operator and machine run concurrently.
Standard machine time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. Used for machine capacity planning and OEE calculations.
Short description of the work to be performed at this operation step within this routing. Describes the specific task (e.g., 'Rough mill housing bore'), not the work center capability in general.
Business identifier for the operation step within the routing (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Determines the display and reference sequence of operations. Corresponds to SAP PP field VORNR.
Standard machine or processing time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. Used for capacity planning and standard cost calculation.
Scheduling sequence number defining the execution order of operations within the routing. Used for parallel or alternative sequences. Corresponds to SAP PP field PLFL.

Standard setup or changeover time in minutes required to prepare the work center for this specific operation within this routing. This value is operation-specific and may differ from the work center default.
Standard value key defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) are relevant for this operation and how they are used in costing and scheduling formulas.
Unique identifier for the shift definition record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Links shift to its site location, enabling shift‑level labor cost and safety incident aggregation per plant.
Reference to the specific work center or production line where this shift is applicable. Nullable if the shift applies plant-wide. Used for capacity planning and machine-specific scheduling.
Sum of all scheduled break periods (lunch, rest breaks) within the shift, in minutes. Subtracted from gross duration to calculate net available time for production.
Textual description of the break structure, including start times and durations (e.g., '10:00-10:15 (15 min), 12:00-12:30 (30 min)'). Used for communication to shop floor personnel and HMI display.

Standard time reserved within the shift for product changeovers, tool changes, or setup activities. Used to adjust net available time for multi-product shifts and to calculate planned downtime for OEE.
Date and time when this shift definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Standard number of operators or workers assigned to this shift pattern. Used for labor capacity planning and cost allocation. Nullable if crew size varies by work center.
Comma-separated list of weekday codes (MON, TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT, SUN) indicating which days this shift pattern applies to. Used for weekly capacity planning and workforce rostering.
Date after which this shift definition is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended shift patterns. Used to retire obsolete shift definitions while preserving historical data.
Date from which this shift definition becomes active and can be used for production scheduling. Supports versioning of shift patterns over time.
Time of day when the shift ends, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). May be less than start_time for shifts that cross midnight. Used to calculate gross shift duration.
Total elapsed time of the shift from start to end, in minutes, including all breaks and non-productive time. Used as the denominator for availability calculations.

Planned overlap time between consecutive shifts for handover communication, equipment status review, and safety briefing. Subtracted from net available time if included in shift boundaries. Nullable for non-continuous operations.
Indicates whether this shift is part of a 24/7 continuous manufacturing operation (True) or a discrete batch operation (False). Used to determine handover requirements and WIP tracking rules.
Multiplier applied to base labor rates for this shift type (e.g., 1.0 for regular, 1.5 for overtime, 2.0 for holiday). Used for labor cost calculation and variance analysis.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Date and time when this shift definition record was last updated. Used for audit trail and to track the currency of master data.

Net productive time available for manufacturing execution, calculated as gross_duration_minutes minus break_duration_minutes. Used as the planned production time for OEE availability calculation and takt time planning.
Free-text field for additional information about the shift pattern, special instructions, or exceptions. Used for communication to planners and supervisors.
Count of distinct break periods scheduled within the shift (e.g., 2 for a shift with one lunch break and one rest break). Used for workforce scheduling and labor compliance reporting.
Target production volume for this shift, in base unit of measure. Used as the denominator for performance OEE calculation and as input to takt time calculation. Nullable for non-production shifts.
Numeric priority ranking for shift selection when multiple shift definitions are valid for the same time period (lower number = higher priority). Used by APS systems to resolve scheduling conflicts.

Description of the multi-day or multi-week rotation cycle this shift belongs to (e.g., '2-2-3 Continental', '4-on-4-off', 'DuPont 12-hour'). Used for long-term workforce scheduling and fatigue management.
Ordinal position of this shift within a daily rotation (e.g., 1 for first shift, 2 for second shift, 3 for third shift). Used for shift handover reporting and continuous production tracking.

Broad categorization of shift purpose. Production shifts are used for manufacturing execution and OEE calculation; non-production shifts (e.g., maintenance, training) are excluded from throughput metrics; mixed shifts include both activities.
Unique business identifier for the shift pattern (e.g., DAY_1, NIGHT_A, SWING_2). Used as the externally-known reference in scheduling systems and MES.
Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Morning Shift). Used for display in reports and HMI screens.

Current lifecycle state of the shift definition. Active shifts are available for scheduling; inactive shifts are temporarily disabled; draft shifts are under review; archived shifts are retained for historical reference only.

Classification of the shift pattern indicating the nature of the working period. Regular shifts are standard production periods; overtime, weekend, and holiday shifts may carry premium labor rates; maintenance and emergency shifts are non-production periods.
Time of day when the shift begins, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). Used to calculate shift boundaries for production tracking and OEE reporting.

Target cycle time per unit to meet customer demand, calculated as net_available_minutes * 60 / planned_output_quantity. Used for production pacing and line balancing. Nullable if not applicable to this shift type.

IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used to convert shift boundaries to UTC for global reporting and to handle daylight saving time transitions.
Unit of measure for planned_output_quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Must align with material master UOM for the products manufactured during this shift.
User ID or name of the person who created this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and data governance.

Unit of measure for capacity planning (e.g., machine hours, labor hours, throughput units).
Grouping code for aggregating work centers in capacity planning and leveling activities.

Date until which this work center configuration is effective. Null indicates indefinite validity.

Current lifecycle status of the work center indicating its operational availability.
