Reference to the customer or OEM for whom this FMEA is being prepared. Customer-specific FMEA requirements (e.g., Ford, GM, Stellantis CSRs) may impose additional rating criteria or documentation standards.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Customer-Specific Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: DFMEAs are conducted at the component level. The existing part_number and part_name plain-text fields are denormalized component references; replacing with component_id FK enables component-level risk

Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: APQP mandates FMEA at the configuration level — configurable industrial products (e.g., automation systems with selectable voltage/communication options) have configuration-specific failure modes. FME

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: FMEAs must be reviewed and updated when engineering revisions change part design or process. Linking fmea to engineering_revision ensures DFMEA/PFMEA currency with design changes — a mandatory IATF 16

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: FMEA in industrial manufacturing is performed on specific equipment/machines to analyze failure modes. IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 require FMEA records traceable to the analyzed asset. Quality and mainten

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: FMEAs are conducted within engineering projects as APQP phase deliverables. Linking fmea to engineering_project enables project-level quality risk tracking and APQP gate review reporting — a standard

Reference to the manufacturing process or process step being analyzed in a PFMEA. Links to the process routing or operation master in Siemens Opcenter MES or SAP S/4HANA PP. Applicable primarily for PFMEA type records.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Reference to the product or component that is the subject of this FMEA analysis. Links the FMEA to the product master record managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Reference to the supplier responsible for the component or sub-process being analyzed, when the FMEA covers a supplier-provided item. Links to the supplier master in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM for supplier quality management purposes.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Process FMEAs are conducted at the work center level to identify failure modes for specific manufacturing operations. Work-center-level FMEA is a standard IATF 16949 requirement; fmea.process_step and

AIAG-VDA Action Priority classification (High, Medium, Low) assigned to the failure mode based on the combination of Severity, Occurrence, and Detection ratings. Replaces the legacy RPN-only prioritization approach introduced in the 2019 AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7 Action Priority Table

description: Description of the actual corrective or preventive action implemented to address the failure mode. Documents what was done versus what was recommended, enabling traceability for PPAP and audit purposes. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016

description: Date on which the recommended action was actually implemented and verified as complete. Compared against target_completion_date to measure action closure timeliness. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Date on which the FMEA was formally reviewed and approved by the responsible engineering and quality authority. Required for PPAP submission and regulatory compliance documentation. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

description: System timestamp recording when the FMEA record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Informatica MDM Data Governance

description: Description of existing design verification or process monitoring controls that detect the failure mode or cause before the product reaches the customer. Examples include inspection plans, SPC monitoring, functional testing, and CMM measurement. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 60812:2018

description: Description of existing design or process controls that prevent the failure cause from occurring or reduce the occurrence rate. Examples include design standards, material specifications, process parameters, and mistake-proofing (poka-yoke) devices. Core AIAG-VDA Step 6 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; IEC 60812:2018

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current controls to detect the failure cause or failure mode before the product reaches the customer. A rating of 10 indicates no detection capability; 1 indicates near-certain detection. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Root cause or mechanism that leads to the failure mode. Describes the specific design weakness, process deficiency, or material condition that initiates the failure (e.g., 'insufficient weld penetration', 'incorrect torque specification'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Description of the consequence of the failure mode on the customer, end user, or downstream process. Defines what the customer experiences when the failure occurs (e.g., 'loss of function', 'noise', 'injury risk'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: The specific manner in which the item, component, or process step could potentially fail to perform its intended function. Represents the physical or chemical description of the failure (e.g., 'fracture', 'short circuit', 'dimensional out-of-tolerance'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Number
description: Externally-known, human-readable document control number assigned to the FMEA record in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Used for cross-referencing in engineering change orders (ECOs), PPAP packages, and APQP deliverables.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Numbering Standard

description: Description of the boundaries and scope of the FMEA analysis, including what is included and excluded from the analysis. Defines the system boundary, interfaces, and assumptions. Required for AIAG-VDA Step 2 (Structure Analysis).
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 2; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.2

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Status
description: Current lifecycle state of the FMEA document within the Siemens Teamcenter PLM workflow. Controls whether the FMEA is editable, under review, formally released for production use, or retired.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Workflow States

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Type
description: Classification of the FMEA by analysis scope: DFMEA (Design FMEA) evaluates design-related failure modes; PFMEA (Process FMEA) evaluates manufacturing process failure modes; SFMEA (System FMEA) evaluates system-level interactions; MFMEA (Machinery FMEA) evaluates equipment failure modes. Aligned with AIAG-VDA methodology.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; IEC 60812:2018

description: Description of the intended function or purpose of the item, component, or process step being analyzed. Defines what the element is supposed to do under normal operating conditions. Core AIAG-VDA Step 3 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 3; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.3

description: Date on which the FMEA analysis was formally initiated or first created. Marks the start of the FMEA lifecycle and is used for APQP timing and project milestone tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1; APQP Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Date of the most recent periodic review of the FMEA. FMEAs must be reviewed and updated when design or process changes occur, when field failures are reported, or on a scheduled basis per the quality management system. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3

description: Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood or frequency of the failure cause occurring. A rating of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence; 1 indicates extremely unlikely. Based on historical failure data, process capability (Cp/Cpk), or engineering judgment. Core AIAG-VDA Step 6 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6; IEC 60812:2018; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site where the process or product being analyzed is produced. Used to scope the FMEA to a specific production location and align with site-specific process controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Name or description of the specific manufacturing process step or operation being analyzed in a PFMEA (e.g., 'Welding Station 3', 'CNC Milling Op 20'). Derived from the process routing in SAP S/4HANA PP or Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES Shop Floor Control

description: Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce the severity, occurrence, or detection rating of the failure mode. Drives CAPA initiation and engineering change orders (ECOs). Core AIAG-VDA Step 8 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 10.2.3

description: Indicates whether the failure mode is associated with a regulatory compliance requirement (e.g., CE Marking, UL certification, EPA, OSHA). Failure modes with this flag require documented evidence of control effectiveness in regulatory submissions.
reference: CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC; UL Standards; EPA Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR; IEC 62443

description: Updated AIAG-VDA Action Priority (High, Medium, Low) recalculated after implementation of the recommended action. Confirms whether the residual risk level is acceptable or requires further action.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9 Action Priority Table

description: Updated detection rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects improved detection capability resulting from new controls, inspection methods, or monitoring systems. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.

Updated occurrence rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring following corrective action. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.

Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after implementation of the recommended action. Used to verify that the corrective action achieved the desired risk reduction. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Updated severity rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Severity typically does not change unless a design change eliminates the failure effect. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field for post-action re-evaluation.
Document revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) tracking the version history of the FMEA in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Incremented upon each approved change to the FMEA content.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Version Control

description: Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (S × O × D). Ranges from 1 to 1000. Used to prioritize failure modes for corrective action. Higher RPN indicates greater risk requiring immediate attention. Stored as a business-critical field per AIAG-VDA methodology.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.3

description: Indicates whether the failure mode has a safety-critical or regulatory compliance impact (severity rating 9 or 10). Safety-critical failure modes require mandatory escalation, special controls, and PPAP documentation. Aligned with OSHA and CE Marking requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); OSHA 29 CFR 1910; CE Marking Directive; ISO 45001:2018

description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or downstream process. A rating of 10 indicates a safety-critical or regulatory non-compliance failure; 1 indicates no discernible effect. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity evaluation criteria. Core AIAG-VDA Step 5 field.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this FMEA record was sourced. Supports data lineage and master data management in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. Typical values: Teamcenter (Siemens Teamcenter PLM), SAP_QM (SAP S/4HANA Quality Management), Opcenter (Siemens Opcenter MES), Manual (direct entry).
reference: Informatica MDM Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Architecture

description: Designation indicating whether the failure mode relates to a special product or process characteristic requiring enhanced control. SC = Special Characteristic; CC = Critical Characteristic; KPC = Key Product Characteristic; KCC = Key Control Characteristic. Drives control plan and inspection plan requirements.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3.3; APQP Manual

description: Planned date by which the recommended action must be implemented and verified. Used for action tracking and escalation management in the FMEA review cycle. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

description: Comma-separated list or description of cross-functional team members who participated in the FMEA analysis session. Typically includes representatives from design engineering, manufacturing, quality, and supplier quality. Required for AIAG-VDA Step 1 documentation.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.2

description: Descriptive title of the FMEA document identifying the product, component, or process being analyzed. Used for search, retrieval, and display in PLM and quality management systems.
reference: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

description: System timestamp recording when the FMEA record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Informatica MDM Data Governance

description: Primary key for inspection_characteristic

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Inspection characteristics define the gauge/instrument required for measurement. IATF 16949 MSA (Measurement System Analysis) requires traceability from each inspection characteristic to the specific
Self-referencing FK on inspection_characteristic (parent_inspection_characteristic_id)

Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Inspection characteristics (upper_spec_limit, lower_spec_limit, target_value, tolerance) are directly derived from product specifications. This is a fundamental quality-product relationship: character
Category of the characteristic indicating the nature of the measurement.
Timestamp when the characteristic record was first created.
Impact level of the characteristic on product quality and compliance.
System that records the measurement data (e.g., MES, PLC, manual log).
Date when the characteristic becomes valid for use.
Date when the characteristic is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).
Number of times this characteristic is inspected each production shift.
Unique alphanumeric code assigned by engineering to identify the characteristic across systems.
Detailed description of what the characteristic measures and why it is important.
Human‑readable name of the inspection characteristic used in reports and work instructions.
Method used to capture the characteristic (e.g., manual, automated sensor).
Indicates whether the characteristic must be inspected for every unit.
True if the characteristic is used in SPC calculations (Cp/Cpk).
Current status of the characteristic definition.
Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.
Unit of measure used for the characteristic (e.g., mm, °C, psi).
Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.
Definition of the sampling approach (e.g., 1‑in‑5, 100% inspection).
Origin of the specification limits for the characteristic.
Nominal target value that the characteristic is expected to meet.
Allowed deviation from the target value, expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the characteristic record.
Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.
Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single inspection lot record in the quality execution system. Primary key for the inspection_lot entity.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer Inspection Lot Reporting mandates associating each inspection_lot with the customer_account to deliver lot‑level quality results to the customer.

Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Inspection lots must be traceable to the specific product configuration being produced for defect analysis by configuration variant. Quality reporting on defect rates per configuration (e.g., specific
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost of inspection activities allocated to cost center for cost‑of‑quality reporting; finance tracks these expenses.

Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Outbound delivery inspections and inbound goods-receipt inspection lots are directly triggered by a delivery in SAP QM. Quality teams need delivery_id on inspection_lot to trace which shipment trigger

Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: In SAP QM and industrial manufacturing, a goods issue movement can trigger an outbound inspection lot (e.g., final inspection before shipment). Linking inspection_lot to goods_issue enables quality ga

Reference to the sales order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for customer-specific inspection requirements, pre-shipment inspections, or RMA receipt inspections. Links quality execution to order fulfillment.
reference: SAP SD Sales Order; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3

description: Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the inspection operations, characteristics, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria to be executed for this lot. Aligns with SAP QM inspection plan assignment.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; APQP Control Plan

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: In SAP QM and IATF 16949 manufacturing, every inspection lot is raised against a specific lot/batch. This link enables lot-level quality traceability for PPAP, containment, and disposition decisions.

Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Inspection lots for incoming goods trace back to the material requirement that drove the purchase order, enabling planning-to-quality traceability reporting. In industrial manufacturing MRP/QM integra

Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) record created as a result of this inspection lot's non-conformance or reject disposition. Enables traceability from inspection event to corrective action workflow.
reference: SAP QM Quality Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR/CAPA Process

description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: In MRP-driven manufacturing, inspection lots for incoming goods or production outputs are traceable to the planned order that initiated the requirement. Quality-to-planning traceability reports and MR

Foreign key linking to supplier.po_line_item. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, incoming inspection lots are created at the PO line item level (each line item may have different material, quantity, and quality requirements). Line-item-level lot tracea
Reference to the material or part master record subject to this inspection lot. Links to the product/material master in SAP MM/PLM for BOM and specification retrieval.
reference: SAP MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Reference to the goods receipt document (SAP material document) that triggered this incoming inspection lot. Applicable for inspection types 01 (GR from PO) and 04 (GR from Production Order). Enables traceability from inspection to inventory movement.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Inspection lots are created for production on specific lines; line-level quality performance reporting and SPC require knowing which production line generated the lot. Essential for line-level first-p

Reference to the manufacturing/production order that triggered this inspection lot, applicable for in-process and final inspection types. Links quality execution to production scheduling in SAP PP / Siemens Opcenter MES.
reference: SAP PP Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Reference to the purchase order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for incoming goods receipt inspections from suppliers. Links to SAP MM purchasing documents.
reference: SAP MM Purchase Order; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Foreign key linking to order.order_rma. Business justification: Returned goods under an RMA require a dedicated inspection lot in industrial manufacturing (incoming inspection of returned material). The inspection_lot table carries a denormalized rma_number plain

Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Inspection lots are created per production run in process and discrete manufacturing. Run-level quality disposition and traceability require direct linkage. Enables run-level quality yield reporting a

Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: For serialized industrial products (drives, PLCs, switchgear), inspection lots are raised against individual serial numbers. Serial-level inspection traceability is required for warranty, field servic

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Inspection lot must be linked to the physical storage location for traceability in the warehouse inspection process, required by ISO 9001 audit.
Reference to the supplier or vendor master record from whom the material was received, applicable for incoming inspection lots. Enables supplier quality performance tracking and audit.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; PPAP

description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Incoming inspection lots in industrial manufacturing originate from a specific supplier site. Site-level quality performance tracking (PPM by site, AQL pass rates by site) is a standard SQE activity a
Reference to the work center or production cell where the in-process inspection is performed. Applicable for in-process and milestone inspection types. Links to SAP PP work center master.
reference: SAP PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

description: Reference number of the quality certificate or Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued for this inspection lot upon acceptance. Supports customer documentation requirements and regulatory compliance traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; CE Marking; UL Certification

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Conformance (CoC) or quality certificate must be generated and issued upon acceptance of this inspection lot. Driven by customer contract requirements or regulatory mandates.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; CE Marking; UL Certification; Customer SLA

description: Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was first persisted in the data platform (Silver layer). Used for data lineage and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

description: Total number of defects or non-conformances detected across all inspection characteristics within this lot. Used for defect rate calculation, SPC analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.
reference: SAP QM Defect Recording; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SPC

description: User ID or name of the quality engineer or authorized personnel who recorded the usage decision and disposition for this inspection lot. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 (Roles and Responsibilities)

description: SAP QM usage decision code (e.g., A1=Accept, R1=Reject, R2=Rework, S1=Scrap) providing a standardized coded classification of the disposition decision for system processing and reporting.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision Code; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Final disposition decision made upon completion of inspection results evaluation. Determines the fate of the inspected material: 'accept' = material released to stock/use; 'reject' = material blocked/returned; 'rework' = material sent for corrective rework; 'scrap' = material scrapped; 'conditional_release' = accepted with documented deviations/concessions.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; PPAP

description: Date and time when the usage decision (disposition) was formally recorded in the quality management system. Marks the closure of the active inspection phase and triggers downstream stock posting or NCR creation.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Code or name of the dynamic modification rule applied to automatically adjust the inspection level (normal/tightened/reduced/skip) based on cumulative quality history for this material-supplier or material-plant combination. Aligns with SAP QM dynamic modification.
reference: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Date and time when all inspection activities and results recording were completed for this lot. Combined with inspection_start_timestamp to compute inspection cycle time (Lead Time metric).
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Current inspection severity level applied to this lot based on the supplier or process quality history. 'normal' = standard AQL sampling; 'tightened' = increased scrutiny due to recent failures; 'reduced' = relaxed sampling due to consistent quality; 'skip' = lot accepted without inspection based on skip-lot qualification.
reference: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-1 Switching Rules; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Primary inspection method applied to this lot: 'visual' = visual examination; 'dimensional' = measurement against dimensional tolerances; 'functional' = operational/performance testing; 'destructive' = destructive testing (sample consumed); 'non_destructive' = NDT methods (ultrasonic, X-ray, etc.); 'chemical' = chemical composition analysis; 'electrical' = electrical parameter testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|dimensional|functional|destructive|non_destructive|chemical|electrical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP QM Inspection Method; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 61131

description: Date and time when physical inspection activities commenced for this lot. Used to calculate inspection cycle time and monitor SLA compliance for quality turnaround.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: SAP QM inspection type code classifying the origin and nature of the inspection lot. Standard SAP codes: 01=Goods Receipt from Purchase Order (incoming), 04=Goods Receipt from Production Order (final), 05=Goods Issue, 06=Delivery to Customer, 08=In-Process Inspection, 10=Recurring Inspection, 89=RMA/Customer Return Receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|04|05|06|08|10|89 — promote to reference product for full SAP inspection type catalogue]
reference: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Business-readable classification of the inspection event type: 'incoming' = supplier goods receipt; 'in_process' = mid-production milestone check; 'final' = post-production completion check; 'recurring' = periodic scheduled inspection; 'rma_receipt' = customer return receipt inspection; 'delivery' = pre-shipment inspection; 'goods_issue' = outbound goods inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incoming|in_process|final|recurring|rma_receipt|delivery|goods_issue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; PPAP

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inspection lot by the quality management system (e.g., SAP QM lot number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with shop floor, suppliers, and auditors.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The principal real-world business event timestamp recording when the inspection lot was triggered and created in the quality management system. Distinct from ETL audit timestamps. Aligns with SAP QM lot creation date/time.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Total quantity of material or units submitted for inspection in this lot. Represents the population from which samples are drawn. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot Quantity; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

business_glossary_term: Inspection Lot Quantity Unit of Measure (UoM)
description: Unit of measure for the inspection lot quantity (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, M=Meter, L=Liter, M2=Square Meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure from material master.
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Free-text field for quality engineers to record observations, special conditions, deviations, or contextual notes relevant to this inspection lot that are not captured in structured fields. Supports audit documentation.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: Current workflow state of the inspection lot within the quality execution lifecycle. Drives downstream actions: 'created' = lot opened awaiting release; 'released' = inspection authorized to begin; 'results_recorded' = all characteristic results entered; 'usage_decided' = disposition decision made; 'closed' = lot archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|results_recorded|usage_decided|closed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Indicates whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was automatically or manually triggered as a result of this inspection lot's reject disposition or detected non-conformances. True = NCR created; False = no NCR required.
reference: SAP QM Defect Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR Process

description: Quantity of units or material within the lot that failed to meet specification requirements. Expressed in the same unit of measure as lot_quantity. Used to calculate reject rate and determine disposition scope.
reference: SAP QM Defect Recording; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR

description: Summarized quality outcome of the inspection lot based on all characteristic results evaluated against acceptance criteria. 'passed' = all characteristics within specification; 'failed' = one or more characteristics out of specification; 'conditionally_passed' = passed with documented deviations; 'pending' = results not yet fully recorded.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SPC

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the inspection lot was created and executed. Supports multi-plant quality reporting and compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

description: Target date by which the inspection lot must be completed and a usage decision recorded, as defined by the inspection plan or production schedule. Supports SLA monitoring and escalation management.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Engineering revision or drawing revision level of the material or part at the time of inspection. Ensures inspection results are traceable to the correct design revision, supporting Engineering Change Order (ECO) and ECN management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP QM; ECO/ECN Process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Code or name of the sampling procedure applied to determine the sample size for this inspection lot (e.g., AQL 1.0, AQL 2.5, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Derived from the inspection plan sampling scheme.
reference: SAP QM Sampling Procedure; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Actual number of units or quantity drawn from the lot for physical inspection, as determined by the sampling procedure in the inspection plan. May differ from the planned sample size if adjustments were made.
reference: SAP QM Sample Management; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the inspection plan record. Primary key for the inspection_plan data product in the quality domain.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan (PLKO/PLMK); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑Specific Inspection Plan process requires linking each inspection_plan to the owning customer_account for contract compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Inspection plans are created for each component to define inspection characteristics; linking ensures traceability from plan to the exact component inspected.

Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: APQP/PPAP requires configuration-specific inspection plans — a 480V drive variant has different inspection criteria than a 240V variant. Quality engineers must link plans to the exact product configur
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planning costs for inspections are budgeted to a cost center; required for inspection budgeting and variance analysis.

Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Inspection plans reference engineering drawings for dimensional characteristics and GD&T callouts. This is a fundamental industrial manufacturing practice — inspection plans cite the governing drawing

Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: When an ECO changes a component, the associated inspection plan must be revised. The existing eco_number plain-text field is a denormalized ECO reference; replacing it with eco_id FK enforces referent

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Inspection plans must be updated when engineering revisions change part tolerances or specifications. Linking inspection_plan to engineering_revision is a core APQP/PPAP traceability requirement — any

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Inspection plans are derived directly from engineering specifications defining tolerances, acceptance criteria, and test methods. APQP and PPAP require inspection plans to reference the governing engi

Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: inspection_plan carries fmea_reference as a plain STRING field referencing the FMEA that drove the inspection plan's characteristic definitions and control limits. This is a denormalized text referenc

Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Inspection plans are plant-specific in SAP QM and industrial QMS systems. inspection_plan.plant_code is a denormalized plain text reference to the plant. Proper FK enables plant-level inspection plan

Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection plans are defined per product SKU for quality control; linking enables plan retrieval by SKU in the Inspection Planning process.

Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Customer-specific inspection plans in industrial manufacturing are contractually mandated by SLA agreements specifying AQL levels, PPAP requirements, and APQP phases. Linking inspection_plan to sla_ag

Identifier of the supplier to whom this inspection plan applies, for incoming or supplier-specific inspection plans. Relevant for receiving inspection and supplier quality audit plans managed via SAP Ariba.
reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; SAP QM Vendor; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

description: Date and time when the inspection plan was formally approved for use. Provides an audit trail for ISO 9001 document control and regulatory compliance.

APQP phase during which this inspection plan was developed or is applicable. Phase 1: Plan and Define; Phase 2: Product Design and Development; Phase 3: Process Design and Development; Phase 4: Product and Process Validation; Phase 5: Feedback, Assessment and Corrective Action.
Acceptable Quality Level expressed as a percentage defective, defining the maximum tolerable defect rate for the sampling plan. Used in conjunction with ISO 2859-1 / ANSI Z1.4 sampling tables.
reference: ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; SAP QM Sampling Scheme

description: Code identifying the measurement or control method used during inspection (e.g., visual, dimensional, functional, chemical, SPC chart). References the SAP QM master inspection characteristic control indicator.
reference: SAP QM QPAM.PRFME; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SPC

description: Document number of the associated APQP Control Plan that this inspection plan implements. The control plan defines the process controls and reaction plans linked to inspection characteristics.
reference: AIAG APQP Control Plan; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index) value required for this inspection characteristic. Typically 1.33 for standard processes and 1.67 for safety-critical characteristics per PPAP requirements.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; APQP; SAP QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Cp/Cpk

description: Date and time when the inspection plan record was first created in the source system. Provides audit trail for data governance and ISO 9001 document control.

Indicates whether this inspection plan was created or modified to satisfy a specific customer quality requirement (CSR) beyond standard internal or regulatory requirements. Relevant for OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) supply relationships.
Date from which this inspection plan version becomes valid and applicable for production inspection operations. Aligns with SAP QM validity start date.

Date on which this inspection plan version expires and is no longer valid for new inspection lots. Null indicates the plan is open-ended with no scheduled expiry. Aligns with SAP QM validity end date.
Category of measurement equipment or test instrument required to perform the inspection. Used for calibration scheduling and resource allocation in Maximo Asset Management.
reference: SAP QM Equipment Assignment; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025

description: Reference to the standardized inspection method or test procedure document used to perform the inspection. Links to the SAP QM inspection method master record.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Method (QPAM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

description: Defines the intensity of inspection applied: full (normal), reduced (lower frequency for trusted suppliers/processes), tightened (increased scrutiny after failures), or skip-lot (periodic sampling). Aligned with SPC-driven dynamic inspection level adjustments.
reference: ISO 2859-1 Switching Rules; SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection is performed. Incoming covers receiving inspection; in-process covers WIP (Work In Progress) checks; final covers end-of-line; outgoing covers pre-shipment; skip indicates skip-lot logic.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Stage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 and 8.6; AIAG PPAP

description: Date and time when the inspection plan record was last updated in the source system. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental lakehouse ingestion.

Detailed narrative description of the inspection plan scope, special instructions, safety precautions, and any additional context required by the inspector. Corresponds to SAP QM long text on the task list header.
reference: SAP QM Task List Long Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

description: Lower specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values below this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.
reference: SAP QM QPAM.UNTOL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

description: Date by which the inspection plan must be reviewed and revalidated to ensure continued suitability and compliance with current product specifications and regulatory requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Internal Quality Management Procedure

description: Routing operation number within the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM task list operation (PLPO.VORNR). Defines the sequence of inspection steps within the plan.
reference: SAP QM PLPO.VORNR; SAP PP Routing

description: Human-readable name or short description of the inspection plan, identifying the product, process, or operation it governs.
Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the inspection plan, aligned with SAP QM plan group and plan group counter. Used for cross-system reference and document control.

Current lifecycle state of the inspection plan. Controls whether the plan is available for use in production inspection operations. Draft plans are under development; active plans are approved for use; obsolete plans are superseded.

Classification of the inspection plan by its operational purpose. Covers in-process, final inspection, receiving inspection, supplier audit, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), and first article inspection types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_process|final_inspection|receiving|supplier_audit|ppap|apqp|first_article — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP QM Inspection Type (PLKO.INSPK); AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; APQP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Version or revision number of the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM plan group counter. Tracks engineering changes and revisions aligned with ECO/ECN processes.
reference: SAP QM PLKO.ZAEHL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ECO/ECN process

description: PPAP submission level (1–5) required for this inspection plan, per AIAG PPAP standard. Level 1 requires only a Part Submission Warrant; Level 3 is the default full submission; Level 5 requires records reviewed at the manufacturing site.
SAP material group or product family code to which this inspection plan applies. Enables grouping of inspection plans by product category for reporting and analytics.
reference: SAP MM MARA.MATKL; SAP QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Description of the reason for the current plan version, such as an ECO (Engineering Change Order), CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) outcome, customer complaint, or regulatory update.
reference: SAP QM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ECO/ECN

description: Number of units to be drawn from the inspection lot for evaluation. Determined by the sampling procedure and AQL level. A value of zero indicates 100% inspection.
reference: SAP QM QPQMEL.STPRO; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Unit of measure for the sample size quantity (e.g., EA for each/piece, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|M|L|M2|M3 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1

description: Code referencing the statistical sampling procedure applied in this inspection plan, including sample size, acceptance number, and rejection number per AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) tables.
reference: SAP QM Sampling Procedure (QPQMEL); ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) charting and Cp/Cpk capability monitoring are activated for this inspection plan. When true, measurement results feed into control charts and process capability analysis.
reference: SAP QM SPC Indicator; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

description: Nominal or target value for the primary inspection characteristic. Represents the ideal measurement around which tolerances are defined. Used as the centerline reference in SPC control charts.
reference: SAP QM QPAM.SOLLWERT; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC

description: Upper specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values exceeding this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.
reference: SAP QM QPAM.OBTOL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

description: SAP QM usage decision code that defines the default disposition action (accept, reject, rework) applied when inspection results meet or fail acceptance criteria.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision (QMEL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Identifier of the work center or production cell where the inspection operation is performed. Corresponds to SAP PP/QM work center assignment on the inspection routing.
reference: SAP QM PLPO.ARBID; SAP PP Work Center; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: User ID of the quality engineer or planner who originally created this inspection plan record in SAP QM or the source system.
Unique surrogate identifier for each inspection characteristic result record within an inspection lot. Primary key for the inspection_result data product in the quality domain.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: ISO 9001/IATF 16949 require measurement results to be traceable to the calibrated instrument used. Linking inspection_result to the specific calibration_record used during measurement is a mandatory m
Reference to the measurement equipment or gauge used to capture the inspection result. Supports gauge R&R studies and calibration traceability per MSA requirements.
reference: SAP PM Equipment (EQUI); Maximo Asset Management; ISO/IEC 17025; IATF 16949 MSA

description: Reference to the master inspection characteristic definition (e.g., dimension, hardness, visual attribute) as configured in the inspection plan. Enables aggregation of results across lots for SPC analysis.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic (QPCD); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Reference to the parent inspection lot under which this characteristic result was recorded. Links the result to its originating inspection event in SAP QM.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Lot (QALS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the characteristic being measured. Ties the result back to the approved quality plan and sampling procedure.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan (QPAM); APQP Control Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Inspection results must be traceable to the specific lot/batch for PPAP documentation, SPC analysis by batch, and customer-specific traceability requirements (IATF 16949). batch_number on inspection_r
Reference to the material or product being inspected. Enables quality performance analysis by SKU, part number, or finished good across production runs.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MARA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: inspection_result currently carries ncr_number as a plain STRING reference to the NCR triggered by an out-of-spec or out-of-control measurement. This is a denormalized text reference that should be re
Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Required for linking each inspection result to its specific order line, enabling quality reports and order fulfillment decisions.

Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: PPAP submissions require inspection results traceable to the specific product revision inspected. revision_level is a denormalized plain attribute on inspection_result; replacing it with a proper FK t
Reference to the manufacturing production order associated with the inspected batch or lot. Enables traceability from quality result back to the production execution event.
reference: SAP PP Production Order (AUFK); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Supplier Performance Report linking inspection results to the purchase order that supplied the material, enabling root‑cause analysis per supplier.
Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Run‑level quality reports aggregate inspection results per production run for OEE and compliance dashboards.

Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: End-of-line test results for serialized automation equipment must link to the specific serialized unit for warranty claims, field failure analysis, and customer-specific serial traceability. serial_nu
Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded for each inspected product SKU; linking allows quality dashboards to aggregate results by SKU.
Reference to the manufacturing work center or production cell where the inspection was performed. Supports OEE and quality-by-location analysis.
reference: SAP PP Work Center (CRHD); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Pass/fail judgment for attribute-type inspection characteristics (e.g., visual defect present/absent, functional test pass/fail). Null for variable characteristics where measured_value is populated.
reference: SAP QM Attribute Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Process capability index Cp measuring the ratio of the specification width to the process spread (6-sigma). Indicates whether the process is capable of meeting specification limits regardless of centering. Cp ≥ 1.33 is typically required per PPAP.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Process capability index Cpk measuring the ratio of the nearest specification limit to the process mean, accounting for process centering. Cpk ≥ 1.33 is typically required for PPAP approval. Key metric for supplier quality and process performance reporting.
reference: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; PPAP 4th Edition

description: Timestamp when this inspection result record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking per lakehouse governance requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Standardized defect classification code identifying the type of nonconformance detected (e.g., DIM-OOT for out-of-tolerance dimension, SURF-SCR for surface scratch). Feeds Pareto analysis and CAPA root cause categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for defect code catalog]
reference: SAP QM Defect Code (Q-Notification); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; PFMEA defect taxonomy

description: Number of defects or nonconformances detected for this characteristic within the inspected sample. Used for defect rate calculation, Pareto analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.
reference: SAP QM Defect Recording (QF01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Free-text description of the nonconformance or defect observed during inspection. Provides context beyond the defect code for root cause analysis and CAPA documentation.
reference: SAP QM Q-Notification Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA process

description: Calendar date on which the inspection was performed. Used for daily/weekly quality trend reporting and shift-level aggregation without requiring timestamp precision.

Method used to perform the inspection: 'manual' (operator measurement), 'automated' (CMM or inline sensor), 'semi_automated', 'destructive' (material consumed in test), or 'non_destructive' (NDT methods such as ultrasonic, X-ray). Affects result reliability and sampling plan selection.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Method; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO/IEC 17025; ASTM NDT Standards

description: Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection was performed: 'incoming' (goods receipt), 'in_process' (during manufacturing), 'final' (finished goods), 'outgoing' (pre-shipment), or 'supplier' (at supplier site).
reference: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 and 8.6; PPAP

description: Date and time when the inspection measurement was physically recorded. The principal business event timestamp for this result. Used for SPC time-series analysis, shift-based quality reporting, and audit trail.
reference: SAP QM Results Recording Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; Siemens Opcenter MES

description: Indicates whether the measurement triggered an SPC out-of-control signal (e.g., point beyond control limits, run rule violation per Western Electric rules). Distinct from out-of-spec: a process can be in-spec but out-of-control. Triggers SPC investigation workflow.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Indicates whether the measured value falls outside the engineering specification limits (USL or LSL). True when measured_value > upper_spec_limit or measured_value < lower_spec_limit. Used for rapid filtering of nonconforming results and NCR triggering.
The lower statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts to detect process shifts.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2 Control Charts; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The lower engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements below this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.
reference: SAP QM LSL; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

description: The actual numeric measurement recorded for a variable inspection characteristic (e.g., 24.987 mm for a diameter). Null for attribute-type characteristics. Core data point for SPC charting and Cp/Cpk calculation.
reference: SAP QM Results Recording (QE01); SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The engineering design target or nominal value for the characteristic as specified in the drawing or specification. Used to calculate deviation from target and for SPC centering analysis.
reference: SAP QM Inspection Plan Nominal; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; GD&T ASME Y14.5

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the inspection was performed. Enables multi-plant quality benchmarking and regulatory reporting by site.

Free-text remarks or observations entered by the inspector at the time of measurement. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields, such as environmental conditions, equipment anomalies, or visual observations.
reference: SAP QM Long Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Current disposition status of the inspection characteristic result: 'accepted' (within specification), 'rejected' (out of specification), 'conditional' (requires further review or deviation approval), 'open' (not yet evaluated), or 'cancelled'.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision (UD); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

description: Number of units or specimens included in the inspection sample for this characteristic result. Determined by the sampling plan (e.g., AQL per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 or Z1.9). Required for statistical validity assessment.
reference: SAP QM Sample Size; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; ISO 2859-1

description: Sampling procedure or plan applied for this inspection characteristic (e.g., AQL 1.0 Level II, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Determines the statistical confidence of the inspection result.
reference: SAP QM Sampling Procedure; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Production shift during which the inspection was performed (e.g., day, afternoon, night). Enables shift-based quality performance analysis to identify systematic variation by shift.
reference: SAP PP Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Type of SPC control chart applied to this characteristic: 'xbar_r' (X-bar and R for subgroups), 'i_mr' (Individuals and Moving Range), 'p_chart' (proportion defective), 'np_chart' (number defective), 'c_chart' (count of defects), 'u_chart' (defects per unit). Determines the statistical method for control limit calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xbar_r|xbar_s|i_mr|p_chart|np_chart|c_chart|u_chart — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 7870-3; ISO 7870-4

description: Sequential subgroup number within the SPC control chart for this characteristic. Used to order data points on the SPC chart and identify the rational subgroup to which this measurement belongs.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

description: Engineering unit of the measured value (e.g., mm, kg, MPa, Ra, °C, %). Follows ISO 80000 and SAP UoM conventions. Required for dimensional analysis and SPC chart labeling.
reference: SAP QM UoM; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inspection result record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: The upper statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Triggers investigation when exceeded even if within specification. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts.
reference: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2 Control Charts; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

description: The upper engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements above this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.
reference: SAP QM USL; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

description: SAP QM usage decision code indicating the final disposition of the inspected material based on this and other characteristic results: 'accept' (release to stock), 'reject' (return or scrap), 'rework' (send for rework), 'scrap', or 'conditional_release' (deviation approval). Drives inventory posting in SAP MM.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision (QA11); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 and 8.7

business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Plan Characteristic Assignment Id
description: Primary key for the plan_characteristic_assignment association

business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Inspection Characteristic Id
description: Foreign key linking to the master inspection characteristic being assigned to the plan.

business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Inspection Plan Id
description: Foreign key linking to the parent inspection plan that this characteristic assignment belongs to.

Total number of inspection characteristics (measurement parameters) defined within this inspection plan. Provides a quick summary of plan complexity for scheduling and resource planning. [Moved from inspection_plan: This is a derived aggregate (count of assigned characteristics) that should be computed from the association table rather than stored as a denormalized field on the plan header. Storing it on the plan creates a maintenance burden and risks inconsistency with the actual assignment records.]

Unit of measure for the inspection characteristic tolerance limits and target value (e.g., mm, µm, N, bar, °C, %). Aligns with SAP QM master inspection characteristic unit. [Moved from inspection_plan: The unit of measure for tolerance limits is a property of the specific characteristic being measured, not of the plan as a whole. A plan covers many characteristics with different units (mm, °C, N·m). This attribute belongs on the assignment or on the characteristic master, not on the plan header. If it represents a plan-level default, it is misleading; if it represents a specific characteristic's unit, it belongs on the assignment.]
reference: SAP QM QPAM.MSEHI; ISO 80000-1; SAP T006

description: Overrides the default inspection method defined on the master characteristic for this plan. For example, a final inspection plan may require CMM measurement where the in-process plan uses a manual gauge. Null if the master control method applies.

Indicates whether this characteristic must be measured for every unit inspected under this plan. A characteristic may be mandatory in one plan (safety-critical context) and optional in another (reduced inspection scope). Cannot reside on the characteristic master because the obligation is plan-specific.

Overrides the default sampling plan defined on the master inspection_characteristic for this specific plan context. For example, a PPAP plan may require 100% inspection of a characteristic that is normally sampled 1-in-5 in routine production. Null if the characteristic's default sampling plan applies.

Defines the ordered position of this characteristic within the inspection plan's operation sequence. Corresponds to the SAP QM characteristic sequence within a plan operation. Required for structured work instruction generation and operator guidance.

Overrides the master characteristic's default tolerance value for this specific plan. Allows tightening of tolerances for customer-specific or PPAP requirements without modifying the master characteristic definition. Null if the master tolerance applies.
Unique identifier for the demand forecast record. Primary key for the demand forecast entity.
Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific demand forecasts are generated per account to support sales‑aligned planning and KPI reporting.

Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers forecast demand at the customer ship-to site level for large accounts with multiple plants. Site-level demand forecasting drives logistics capacity planning and inventory posi

Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In configure-to-order industrial manufacturing, demand forecasts are created at the configuration level (e.g., specific voltage/power variant of a drive system) to support S&OP and ATP processes. Plan
Reference to the forecast version record enabling scenario comparison and version lineage tracking.

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand forecasts in industrial manufacturing are generated at the product family level using statistical models, then disaggregated to SKU. This FK supports family-level forecast accuracy reporting, S
Reference to the material or item being forecasted. Links to the material master record in inventory domain.

Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: S&OP process in industrial manufacturing explicitly reconciles demand forecasts against the sales opportunity pipeline. Planners need to trace which opportunity seeded a forecast entry for pipeline-ba

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Demand forecasting in industrial manufacturing is structured by customer segment (OEM, distributor, process industry). Planners generate and review segment-level forecasts for capacity and supply plan
Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Forecasting uses the SKU master to align demand quantities with product specifications and pricing.
Reference to the planning location, warehouse, or distribution center for which demand is forecasted.
Comments or notes provided by the approver during the forecast approval workflow, documenting decision rationale or conditions.
Systematic forecast bias expressed as percentage, indicating tendency to over-forecast (positive) or under-forecast (negative). Used to detect and correct systematic errors.
Lower bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the pessimistic demand scenario for risk analysis and safety stock planning.
Upper bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the optimistic demand scenario for capacity planning and supply risk assessment.
Statistical confidence level of the forecast interval expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence). Used for supply planning sensitivity analysis.
Date when the forecast achieved consensus approval in the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) process, marking it as the agreed demand plan.
Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Classification of demand type: independent (end-customer driven, forecasted) or dependent (derived from BOM and parent item demand via MRP explosion).
Characterization of the historical demand behavior pattern used to select appropriate forecasting models and safety stock strategies.
Historical accuracy percentage of the forecast model for this material-location combination, calculated as (1 - MAPE) * 100. Used for model selection and tuning.
Indicates whether this forecast is subject to consumption by actual customer orders in the MRP logic. Controls forecast vs. order-driven planning behavior.
Timestamp when the forecast was generated by the forecasting engine or planning system. Represents the business event time of forecast creation.
Number of days into the future that this forecast covers, calculated from the planning period start date. Used for lead time coverage analysis.
Code identifying the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, EXP_SMOOTH, MOVING_AVG, ML_ENSEMBLE).
Human-readable name of the forecasting model or algorithm applied to generate this forecast.
Business identifier for the forecast record, externally visible and used for tracking and reference in planning meetings.
Predicted demand quantity for the material at the specified location during the planning period. Core forecast value used for MRP and capacity planning.
Current lifecycle status of the forecast record in the approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval or rejection.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this forecast record, tracking manual adjustments or workflow updates.
MAPE metric measuring forecast error as a percentage of actual demand. Lower values indicate better forecast accuracy. Key KPI for demand planning performance.
MRP area code defining the planning scope and organizational unit for material requirements planning execution.
Free-text notes or comments about this forecast record, capturing planner insights, assumptions, or special considerations.
Indicates whether this forecast was flagged as a statistical outlier requiring review. Used for data quality monitoring and exception management.
Explanation for why this forecast was flagged as an outlier, documenting the business or statistical reason for the exception.
Code identifying the planning group or demand planner responsible for this forecast. Used for workload distribution and accountability.
End date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the end of the demand horizon.
Start date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the beginning of the demand horizon.
Current stage of the product in its lifecycle. Influences forecasting model selection and demand pattern expectations.
Indicates whether this forecast includes promotional or campaign-driven demand uplift. Used to separate base demand from promotional spikes.
Expected percentage increase in demand due to promotional activities during the planning period. Used for promotional planning and inventory pre-positioning.
Manual adjustment quantity applied by sales team to the statistical baseline forecast, reflecting market intelligence and customer commitments.
Business justification for the sales adjustment, documenting market intelligence, customer commitments, or promotional activities driving the override.
Name of the planning scenario this forecast belongs to (e.g., Base Case, Optimistic, Pessimistic, New Product Launch). Enables scenario comparison and sensitivity analysis.
Seasonal adjustment factor applied to the forecast for this planning period. Values above 1.0 indicate peak season, below 1.0 indicate off-season.
Code identifying the source system that generated or provided this forecast record (e.g., D365_SCM, SAP_APO, CUSTOM_MODEL).
Trend component coefficient from the forecasting model, indicating the rate of demand growth or decline over time.
Unit of measure for the forecast quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Must align with material master base UOM.
Classification of the forecast version: baseline (statistical model output), sales_adjusted (sales team input), consensus (S&OP aligned), approved (final for MRP), or working (draft).
Unique identifier for the demand plan version record. Primary key for the demand plan version entity.
Reference to the parent demand plan that this version belongs to. Links to the master demand plan entity.

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand plan versions in industrial manufacturing S&OP are created and managed at the product family level (family-level statistical forecasting is standard). This FK enables S&OP teams to aggregate de
Reference to the newer demand plan version that replaces this version. Null if this is the current active version. Maintains version lineage and audit trail for plan evolution tracking.

Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Demand plan versions are plant-scoped in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on demand_plan_version is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level demand planning ve
Date when this demand plan version was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Null if version has not yet been approved. Marks the transition to executable plan status.
Indicates whether this demand plan version has received formal approval through the S&OP governance process. True if approved for execution, false if still in draft or review.

Indicates whether this version serves as the baseline reference for variance analysis and scenario comparison. True if this is the authoritative baseline, false for alternative scenarios or working versions.

Systematic tendency of this demand plan version to over-forecast (positive bias) or under-forecast (negative bias) actual demand. Calculated as the sum of forecast errors divided by sum of actuals, used to detect and correct persistent forecasting errors.

Statistical confidence level associated with the demand forecast in this version. Represents the probability that actual demand will fall within the forecasted range, typically 80%, 90%, or 95% confidence intervals used for safety stock calculations.
System timestamp when this demand plan version record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.

Number of days from the current date within which demand changes are frozen or restricted. Defines the period where MRP (Material Requirements Planning) will not automatically adjust planned orders based on forecast changes, protecting near-term production stability.

Measured accuracy of this demand plan version compared to actual demand realization. Calculated as 100 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), used for continuous improvement of forecasting methods and model selection.

Identifier for the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate demand forecasts in this version (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, machine learning model). Applicable when planning method is statistical or APS.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM - Demand Forecasting Models

description: System timestamp when this demand plan version record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.
Product family or material group classification for demand aggregation. Used to organize demand planning at the product category level before disaggregation to individual SKUs (Stock Keeping Units).
Total number of calendar days covered by this demand plan version. Calculated as the difference between planning horizon end and start dates, used for capacity planning and lead time analysis.
Last date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the end of the forecast window, typically aligned with fiscal quarters or annual planning cycles.
First date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the beginning of the forecast window for supply planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.

Methodology used to generate this demand plan version. Statistical uses algorithmic forecasting, collaborative incorporates cross-functional input, consensus reflects S&OP agreement, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) uses optimization engines, and manual indicates planner-driven creation.

Number of days from the current date within which supply planning changes require manual approval. Defines the period where automated MRP adjustments are restricted to prevent disruption to committed production schedules.

Business name for the planning scenario represented by this version. Used for what-if analysis and sensitivity testing in S&OP processes (e.g., Optimistic Growth, Conservative Base, Supply Constrained).

Identifier for the operational system that created or owns this demand plan version record. Typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM, SAP APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization), or specialized demand planning applications for data lineage and integration traceability.
Aggregate demand quantity across all materials and time buckets within this plan version. Represents the total forecasted demand volume for supply network capacity planning and scenario comparison.
Standard unit of measure for the total planned demand quantity. Typically base inventory unit (EA, KG, L, M) aligned with material master UOM definitions in SAP MM or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.

Free-text commentary and business context for this demand plan version. Captures assumptions, market conditions, promotional events, supply constraints, or other factors influencing the forecast that are relevant for scenario interpretation and decision-making.
Business identifier for the demand plan version. Human-readable version code used for tracking and referencing specific plan iterations (e.g., V1.0, V2.1, BASELINE-2024Q1).
Current lifecycle status of the demand plan version. Tracks the approval workflow state from initial draft through review, approval, and eventual archival or supersession by newer versions.

Classification of the demand plan version indicating its purpose and stage in the S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) process. Baseline represents initial statistical forecast, sales-adjusted includes sales team input, consensus reflects cross-functional agreement, approved is the final authorized plan, and working is an in-progress draft.
Date when this demand plan version was created. Represents the business event timestamp for version instantiation in the S&OP cycle.

Code indicating special procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or third-party order processing.

Unique identifier for the MRP/MRP II planning run execution. Primary key.
